candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Kahne glycosidation describing activation of glycosyl sulfoxides with triflic anhydride or related activators to generate glycosyl triflates that react with O-, S-, or N-nucleophiles with high anomeric control.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: sulfoxide method; glycosyl triflate formation; activation by Tf2O, TMSOTf, or TfOH with bases such as DTBMP/TTBP; participation of neighboring groups; and low-temperature coupling to alcohol, phenol, thiol, or amide nucleophiles.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Keck asymmetric allylation describing catalytic enantioselective addition of allylstannanes or allylsilanes to aldehydes under BINOL/Ti(IV) Lewis-acid catalysis to form chiral secondary homoallylic alcohols.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: in situ assembly of Ti(Oi-Pr)4/BINOL catalysts, use of molecular sieves, aldehyde allylation with allyltributylstannane, enantioselective transition-state models, and catalyst variants derived from BINOL and diamine ligands.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Keck macrolactonization describing high-dilution cyclization of hydroxy acids using carbodiimide activation with DMAP and DMAP·HCl or EDCI to form medium- and large-ring lactones.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: syringe-pump substrate addition under reflux, DCC or EDCI activation, DMAP/DMAP·HCl proton-transfer mediation, suppression of N-acylurea byproducts, and comparison with Corey-Nicolaou and Shiina-type macrolactonizations.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Keck radical allylation describing free-radical substitution of alkyl halides and related radical precursors with allyltributyltin under AIBN, benzoyl peroxide, heat, or photolysis to install allyl groups.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: atom-transfer initiation from alkyl bromides, chlorides, or selenides; radical chain propagation with allyltributyltin; chemoselective allylation of halides over many functional groups; and mechanistic comparison with earlier Kosugi and Grignon procedures.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Knoevenagel condensation describing base- or amine-catalyzed condensation of aldehydes or ketones with active methylene compounds to furnish alpha,beta-unsaturated carbonyl or related electron-poor alkenes.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: primary, secondary, or tertiary amine catalysis; active methylene donors such as malonates, cyanoacetates, or 1,3-dicarbonyl compounds; dehydration of beta-hydroxyl intermediates; and competition between aldol addition and iminium/enamine pathways.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Koenigs-Knorr glycosidation describing coupling of glycosyl halides with alcohols or phenols under heavy-metal salts, Lewis acids, or phase-transfer conditions to form O-alkyl or aryl glycosides with substrate-controlled stereochemistry.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: preparation and activation of glycosyl chlorides or bromides; neighboring-group participation for beta-selectivity; use of silver or mercury salts and Lewis acids; oxocarbenium/dioxolanium ion reactivity; and limitations caused by elimination or hydrolysis of glycosyl halides.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Kolbe-Schmitt reaction describing carboxylation of phenoxide salts with carbon dioxide under thermal or pressurized conditions to afford ortho- or para-hydroxy aromatic carboxylic acids such as salicylic acid derivatives.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: conversion of phenols to alkali-metal phenoxides, CO2 insertion under heat or pressure, regiochemical control by cation size and substrate substitution, phenoxide-CO2 complex formation, and application to mono- or polyhydric phenols and naphthols.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Kornblum oxidation describing conversion of activated alkyl halides or alpha-halo carbonyl compounds to aldehydes, ketones, or alpha-oxo carbonyl compounds by dimethyl sulfoxide under basic or silver-assisted conditions.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: displacement of halide by DMSO to form alkoxysulfonium salts, deprotonation and [2,3]-sigmatropic or elimination pathways to carbonyl compounds, silver-assisted activation with AgOTs or AgBF4, and Ganem variants employing tertiary amine N-oxides.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Krapcho dealkoxycarbonylation describing salt-promoted cleavage of methoxycarbonyl or ethoxycarbonyl groups from malonic esters, beta-keto esters, alpha-cyano esters, and related electron-poor esters in wet polar aprotic solvents.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: one-pot dealkoxycarbonylation using NaCN, KCN, LiCl, NaCl, or related salts in DMSO, DMF, DMA, or HMPT; dependence on alpha-substitution and ester identity; mechanistic pathways involving cyanide addition or SN2 displacement at alkyl esters; and compatibility with base-sensitive substrates.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Kröhnke pyridine synthesis describing the condensation of pyridinium salts derived from alpha-haloketones with alpha,beta-unsaturated ketones and ammonia equivalents to furnish substituted pyridines.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: preparation of acylmethylpyridinium salts from alpha-haloketones and pyridine; Michael addition to enones; 1,5-diketone formation; cyclization with ammonium acetate or related ammonia surrogates; and direct pyridine formation without separate oxidation.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Kulinkovich reaction describing titanium-mediated conversion of esters or amides into 1-alkylcyclopropanols and cyclopropylamines using Grignard reagents and titanium alkoxides.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: generation of titanacyclopropane from Ti(O-i-Pr)4 and EtMgBr or related Grignard reagents; dialkylation of esters or amides; cis-selective cyclopropane formation; sensitivity to steric demand and substrate class; and catalytic variants using reduced titanium loadings.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Kulinkovich reaction describing titanium-mediated conversion of esters or amides into 1-alkylcyclopropanols and cyclopropylamines using Grignard reagents and titanium alkoxides.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: generation of titanacyclopropane from Ti(O-i-Pr)4 and EtMgBr or related Grignard reagents; dialkylation of esters or amides; cis-selective cyclopropane formation; sensitivity to steric demand and substrate class; and catalytic variants using reduced titanium loadings.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Kumada cross-coupling describing transition-metal-catalyzed union of aryl or alkenyl halides with Grignard or organolithium reagents to form carbon-carbon bonds.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: Ni- or Pd-catalyzed oxidative addition/transmetalation/reductive elimination cycles; stereoretentive coupling of alkenyl halides; dependence of reactivity on ligand and halide type; and tradeoffs between organomagnesium and more reactive organolithium or organoaluminum partners.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Larock indole synthesis describing Pd-catalyzed heteroannulation of o-iodoanilines with internal alkynes to furnish 2,3-disubstituted indoles or fused indole systems.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: oxidative addition of o-iodoanilines, alkyne carbopalladation, regioselectivity governed by alkyne substituent size, chloride additives and base requirements, and modified fused-system variants employing o-iodoaniline-derived imines.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the McMurry coupling describing reductive coupling of aldehydes and ketones with low-valent titanium reagents to form alkenes, including intra- and intermolecular variants.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: low-valent titanium generated from TiCl3 or TiCl4 with reducing metals, pinacolate-like intermediates, deoxygenation to alkenes, and special value for macrocyclizations and sterically hindered olefins.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Meerwein arylation describing addition of aryl radicals derived from aryldiazonium salts to electron-poor olefins, often in the presence of copper salts, to furnish aryl-substituted olefins or related adducts.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: in situ diazotization of anilines, radical aryl addition to activated alkenes, decarboxylative arylation of cinnamic-acid-type substrates, and frequent use of Cu(I)/Cu(II) salts in aqueous or mixed solvents.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Meerwein-Ponndorf-Verley reduction describing reversible transfer-hydrogen reduction of aldehydes and ketones with aluminum alkoxides, typically aluminum isopropoxide, to furnish alcohols under nonacidic conditions.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: six-membered transition state for hydride transfer, reversibility with Oppenauer oxidation, chemoselective reduction of carbonyl groups in the presence of many other functionalities, and common use of aluminum isopropoxide with isopropanol or related secondary alcohols.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Meisenheimer rearrangement describing thermal or oxidative rearrangement of tertiary amine N-oxides to N,O-disubstituted hydroxylamines or ring-expanded oxazacycles via [1,2]- or [2,3]-shifts.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: acyclic and cyclic N-oxide rearrangements, competition between [1,2]- and [2,3]-shifts depending on substitution and beta-hydrogen content, and frequent use of N-oxide generation followed by thermal rearrangement or spontaneous ring expansion.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Meyer-Schuster and Rupe rearrangements describing acid-catalyzed conversion of secondary or tertiary propargylic alcohols into alpha,beta-unsaturated carbonyl compounds.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: protic or Lewis-acid catalysis, Meyer-Schuster rearrangement of secondary/tertiary propargylic alcohols to enones or enals, Rupe rearrangement of tertiary alcohols to alpha,beta-unsaturated ketones, and mechanistic involvement of propargylic/allenyl cation equilibration.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Michael addition describing 1,4-conjugate addition of carbon or heteroatom nucleophiles to activated alkenes or alkynes bearing electron-withdrawing groups.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: conjugate addition to alpha,beta-unsaturated carbonyl and related acceptors, use of stabilized carbon nucleophiles or heteroatom donors, thermodynamic reversibility in protic media, and broad utility of Michael adducts in cascade synthesis.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Midland Alpine-Borane reduction describing asymmetric reduction of ketones and aldehydes with chiral B-3-alpha-pinanyl-9-BBN reagents to give enantioenriched alcohols.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: preparation of Alpine-Borane from alpha-pinene and 9-BBN, chiral hydride delivery to prochiral carbonyl compounds, substrate- and steric-effects on rate and selectivity, and mechanistic transition-state models for enantioselection.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Minisci reaction describing radical alkylation or acylation of protonated electron-deficient heteroarenes under oxidative conditions.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: generation of carbon-centered radicals from diverse precursors, addition to protonated azaarenes, reoxidation to restore aromaticity, and regioselective functionalization of pyridines, quinolines, isoquinolines, and related heterocycles.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Mislow-Evans rearrangement describing allylic sulfoxide/sulfinate-sulfoxide rearrangement chemistry that delivers allylic alcohols through reversible [2,3]-sigmatropic sulfur migration.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: thermal racemization of allylic sulfoxides, conversion of allylic alcohols to allylic sulfoxides and alcohols, stereospecific [2,3]-rearrangement through sulfinate esters, and thiophile-assisted reductive cleavage to allylic alcohol products.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Mitsunobu reaction describing inversion-based substitution of alcohols using phosphine/azodicarboxylate reagents and diverse nucleophiles.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: activation of secondary alcohols by phosphine and dialkyl azodicarboxylates, inversion of configuration, broad oxygen/nitrogen/sulfur/carbon nucleophile scope, and mechanistic participation of zwitterionic phosphonium intermediates.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for Miyaura boration describing palladium-catalyzed conversion of aryl or vinyl halides and triflates into boronic esters using bis(pinacolato)diboron or tetraalkoxydiborons.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: Pd-catalyzed borylation of aryl/vinyl electrophiles, use of B2pin2 or related diborons, importance of acetate base, compatibility with cross-coupling workflows, and catalytic-cycle steps of oxidative addition, transmetalation, and reductive elimination.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Mukaiyama aldol reaction describing Lewis-acid-promoted addition of silyl enol ethers to aldehydes or ketones to form beta-hydroxy carbonyl compounds.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: preformed enol-silane nucleophiles, Lewis acid activation of carbonyl electrophiles, anti/syn stereochemical models, catalytic asymmetric variants, and broad use in fragment coupling for polyketide synthesis.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Myers asymmetric alkylation describing highly diastereoselective alpha-alkylation of pseudoephedrine-derived amide enolates to access optically active carboxylic-acid derivatives and related products.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: pseudoephedrine auxiliary-controlled alkylation, LiCl-assisted enolate generation, broad alkyl halide scope, high alpha-face selectivity, and downstream conversion of alkylated amides into acids, aldehydes, or alcohols.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Nagata hydrocyanation describing conjugate addition of cyanide to activated alkenes or enones using dialkylaluminum cyanides or HCN/AlX3 combinations.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: 1,4-addition of cyanide to alpha,beta-unsaturated ketones and related acceptors, use of Et2AlCN or HCN/AlEt3, strong solvent and Lewis-acid effects, and hydrolysis to beta-cyano carbonyl compounds.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Nazarov cyclization describing acid- or Lewis-acid-promoted electrocyclization of divinyl ketones through pentadienyl cation intermediates to cyclopentenones.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: divinyl ketone precursors, 4π conrotatory electrocyclization, pentadienyl cation intermediates, control by beta-substituents and silicon directing groups, and stereospecific ring closure to cyclopentenones.

candidates.page_summary: Overview page for the Neber rearrangement describing base-induced conversion of O-acylated ketoximes into alpha-amino ketones through azirine intermediates.
candidates.page_notes: Key concepts: preparation of O-acyl ketoximes from oximes, rearrangement under alkoxide base, competing concerted and nitrene pathways, formation of 2H-azirines, and utility for alpha-amino ketone synthesis.
